perturbation.original_text: "Affiliate" means, when used with respect to any specified Person, a Person that directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, such specified Person. For the purposes of this definition, "control" (including the terms "controlled by" and "under common control with") with respect to the Source: LEJU HOLDINGS LTD, DRS (on F-1), 1/21/2014 relationship between or among two or more Persons, means the possession, directly or indirectly or as trustee, personal representative or executor, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the affairs or management of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, as trustee, personal representative or executor, by contract, credit arrangement or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: "Affiliate" means, when used with respect to any specified Person, a Person that directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, such specified Person. For the purposes of this definition, "control" (including the terms "controlled by" and "under common control with") with respect to the relationship between or among two or more Persons, means the possession, directly or indirectly or as trustee, personal representative or executor, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the affairs or management of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, as trustee, personal representative or executor, by contract, credit arrangement or otherwise. **However, for the purposes of Article VIII (Indemnification), control will only be defined as ownership of more than 50% of voting shares.**
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Affiliate" relies on the concept of 'control,' defined broadly as the power to direct management, regardless of ownership. The addition modifies this definition specifically for Article VIII (Indemnification), limiting 'control' to ownership of more than 50% of voting shares. This creates an in-text contradiction because an entity might be considered an affiliate under the general definition, triggering certain obligations, but not an affiliate for indemnification purposes, leading to uncertainty about when indemnification duties apply. The contradiction is present because the definition of affiliate in Article I, is now contradicted by the added modification in Article I.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE VIII INDEMNIFICATION 8.1. Indemnification by Licensor. Licensor shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless Licensee and its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees, agents, shareholders, successors and assigns, (collectively, the "Licensee Parties") from and against any claim, suit, demand or action ("Action"), and any and all direct losses suffered or incurred by Licensee in connection with any third party claims arising out of or resulting from any breach by Licensor of any provision of this Agreement. Licensor's obligation to indemnify Licensee shall be conditioned on (a) Licensee's provision to Licensor of prompt notice of such an Action (except where any delay does not materially prejudice Licensor); (b) Licensee's reasonable cooperation with Licensor in the defense and settlement of such an Action at Licensor's cost; and (c) Licensor having exclusive control of the defense, settlement and/or compromise of such an Action (provided that Licensor may not settle any Action in a manner that adversely affects Licensee without Licensee's prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed). 8.2. Indemnification by Licensee. Licensee shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless Licensor and its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees, agents, shareholders, successors and assigns, (collectively, the "Licensor Parties") from and against any Action, and any and all direct losses suffered or incurred by Licensor in connection with any third party claims (a) arising out of or resulting from any breach by Licensee of any provision of this Agreement, (b) regarding the Content (other than Licensed Content) of the websites associated with Licensed Domain Names, or (c) regarding any Content that was subject to a request for removal by a Governmental Authority, even if Licensee removes such Content within the time period proscribed by the Governmental Authority, provided that, in all cases, Licensee shall not be liable for any direct losses suffered or incurred by Licensor as a result of Licensor's failure to provide Licensee with a reasonable period of time to remove Content in cases where (i) the basis or nature of the offense has not previously been identified by any Governmental Authority as offensive or inappropriate and (ii) Licensee has not also received notice from the Governmental Authority. Licensee's obligation to indemnify Licensor shall be conditioned on (x) Licensor's provision to Licensee of prompt notice of such an Action (except where any delay does not materially prejudice Licensee); (y) Licensor's reasonable cooperation with Licensee in the defense and settlement of such an Action at Licensee's cost; and (z) Licensee having exclusive control of the defense, settlement and/or compromise of such an Action (provided that Licensee may not settle any Action in a manner that adversely affects Licensor without Licensor's prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed).
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a qualification to the definition of 'Affiliate' specifically for Article VIII (Indemnification). This contradicts the general definition of 'Affiliate' established earlier in the document, which is used in Article VIII. The original text defines Affiliate broadly, while the changed text narrows the definition for indemnification purposes. This creates a direct conflict, as an entity might be an affiliate under the original definition, triggering indemnification obligations under Article VIII, but not under the new, limited definition introduced in the changed text. This creates a structural flaw in the text. A reasonable reader would immediately recognize the sections as incompatible, specifically about who would be covered under indemnification, based on which definition of 'Affiliate' is used.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall ensure that the Business complies with all applicable Laws in respect of operation, advertising and promotion of the Business and use of the Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content in connection therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall ensure that the Business complies with all applicable Laws in respect of operation, advertising and promotion of the Business and use of the Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content in connection therewith. **However, Licensor holds full responsibility for compliance with advertising laws and regulations.**
perturbation.explanation: The original clause places the responsibility for compliance with all applicable laws on the Licensee. The added sentence shifts the responsibility for compliance with advertising laws to the Licensor. This creates a direct contradiction regarding responsibility for legal compliance related to advertising, potentially leading to disputes over who bears the liability for any violations. The contradictory effect arises in Section 3.4 where Licensee's overall responsibility is defined, which is directly undermined by the added sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee shall ensure that the Business complies with all applicable Laws in respect of operation, advertising and promotion of the Business and use of the Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content in connection therewith. However, Licensor holds full responsibility for compliance with advertising laws and regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text places the responsibility for compliance with all applicable laws on the Licensee regarding operation, advertising, and promotion. The changed text adds 'However, Licensor holds full responsibility for compliance with advertising laws and regulations,' which directly contradicts the Licensee's overall responsibility for advertising law compliance as defined in the original text. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding which party is liable for advertising compliance, potentially leading to disputes and unenforceability, and aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: All notices, requests, claims, demands and other communications hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given, made or received (i) on the date of delivery if delivered in person or by messenger service, (ii) on the date of confirmation of receipt of transmission by facsimile (or, the first (1 ) Business Day following such receipt if (a) such 11 s t Source: LEJU HOLDINGS LTD, DRS (on F-1), 1/21/2014 date of confirmation is not a Business Day or (b) confirmation of receipt is given after 5:00 p.m., Beijing time) or (iii) on the date of confirmation of receipt if delivered by an internationally recognized overnight courier service or registered or certified mail (or, the first (1 ) Business Day following such receipt if (a) such date of confirmation is not a Business Day or (b) confirmation of receipt is given after 5:00 p.m., Beijing time) to the respective parties hereto at the following addresses (or at such other address for a party as shall be specified in a notice given in accordance with this Section 10.3):
perturbation.changed_text: All notices, requests, claims, demands and other communications hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given, made or received (i) on the date of delivery if delivered in person or by messenger service, (ii) on the date of confirmation of receipt of transmission by facsimile (or, the first (1 ) Business Day following such receipt if (a) such 11 s t Source: LEJU HOLDINGS LTD, DRS (on F-1), 1/21/2014 date of confirmation is not a Business Day or (b) confirmation of receipt is given after 5:00 p.m., Beijing time) or (iii) on the date of confirmation of receipt if delivered by an internationally recognized overnight courier service or registered or certified mail (or, the first (1 ) Business Day following such receipt if (a) such date of confirmation is not a Business Day or (b) confirmation of receipt is given after 5:00 p.m., Beijing time) to the respective parties hereto at the following addresses (or at such other address for a party as shall be specified in a notice given in accordance with this Section 10.3): **Notwithstanding the foregoing, any notice regarding termination of this agreement is valid only if communicated verbally in person.**
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that all notices must be in writing. The added sentence contradicts this by stating that termination notices are valid only if communicated verbally in person. This creates a conflict regarding the proper method for delivering termination notices, making it unclear whether written notice is sufficient. The contradiction is being made due to the inserted text at the end of the original definition of what is being considered a valid notice in Section 10.3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices, requests, claims, demands and other communications hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given, made or received (i) on the date of delivery if delivered in person or by messenger service, (ii) on the date of confirmation of receipt of transmission by facsimile (or, the first (1 ) Business Day following such receipt if (a) such 11 s t Source: LEJU HOLDINGS LTD, DRS (on F-1), 1/21/2014 date of confirmation is not a Business Day or (b) confirmation of receipt is given after 5:00 p.m., Beijing time) or (iii) on the date of confirmation of receipt if delivered by an internationally recognized overnight courier service or registered or certified mail (or, the first (1 ) Business Day following such receipt if (a) such date of confirmation is not a Business Day or (b) confirmation of receipt is given after 5:00 p.m., Beijing time) to the respective parties hereto at the following addresses (or at such other address for a party as shall be specified in a notice given in accordance with this Section 10.3): Notwithstanding the foregoing, any notice regarding termination of this agreement is valid only if communicated verbally in person.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'All notices... shall be in writing'. The changed text adds a sentence stating 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, any notice regarding termination of this agreement is valid only if communicated verbally in person.' This directly contradicts the initial statement that all notices must be in writing, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the entirety of the first sentence of section 10.3, now rendered partially invalid by the added sentence.
